title: Washington SB 5932: Providing Certainty for the Development of Low-to-Zero Carbon Alternative Jet Fuel Production
content: Washington state is reinforcing its position as a global leader in the Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) market with the introduction of Senate Bill 5932. This legislation is the latest power move by the legislature to provide the gold standard of financial and regulatory certainty for the Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) industry. Breaking Status Update: The bill is moving at high speed. After being introduced on January 12, 2026, it has already advanced to a critical milestone. It is scheduled for an executive session in the Senate Committee on Environment, Energy & Technology on February 3, 2026, at 1:30 PM. This is the pivotal moment where the committee will vote on the bill's future. While much of the bill mirrors previous successful policies, Section 2 is the absolute game-changer. It officially codifies how the state calculates the greenness of electricity used in fuel production. Instead of being stuck with a generic, often dirty grid average, producers can now lock in their own low-carbon power through private contracts (PPAs). This means if a plant runs on 100% wind or solar, the law must recognize that zero-carbon score. For investors and developers, this removes the carbon accounting guessing game. It guarantees that the massive energy needs for processes like water electrolysis wonâ€™t dilute a project's environmental value. By aligning the Clean Fuels Program with modern energy procurement, Washington is essentially de-risking billions in potential capital investments through 2037. Combined with the existing $2.00 per gallon tax credit and a slashed 0.275% B&O tax rate, this bill cements Washington as the most attractive place on earth to build a SAF refinery.
author: Analysts Account
